content.problemStatement:
Chef owns a clothing store and has a limited number of t-shirts in different sizes. There are three sizes available:


Small (SSS): Chef has XXX small t-shirts.
Large (LLL): Chef has YYY large t-shirts.
Extra Large (XLXLXL): Chef has ZZZ extra-large t-shirts.
There are AAA people who wear small, BBB people who wear large, and CCC people who wear extra-large t-shirts.

Each person is happy if they receive a t-shirt in their desired size. However, Chef has a magical tailoring machine that allows him to convert larger shirts into smaller ones:


An XLXLXL t-shirt can be converted into an LLL or an SSS t-shirt.
An LLL t-shirt can be converted into an SSS t-shirt.
Conversions are one-way and irreversible.

Each conversion maintains a **1:11:11:1** ratio (i.e., one XLXLXL can become exactly one LLL or one SSS).
Chef wants to distribute the t-shirts optimally, making conversions if necessary, to maximize the number of happy people.

Can you determine the maximum number of people who will be happy?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing six space-separated integers XXX, YYY, ZZZ, AAA, BBB and CCC −-− the number of small t-shirts available, large t-shirts available, extra-large t-shirts available, people who want small t-shirts, people who want large t-shirts, and people who want extra-large t-shirts respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of people who will be happy.
constraints: 0≤X,Y,Z,A,B,C≤50 \leq X, Y, Z, A, B, C \leq 50≤X,Y,Z,A,B,C≤5

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Since each t-shirt matches exactly with each customer’s requirement, all three people are happy.
Test Case 2: Using the magical machine, Chef converts one XLXLXL t-shirt into a SSS and the other into a LLL. This way, both customers get the correct size.
Test Case 3: Only the LLL t-shirt fits directly, and the XLXLXL can be converted into a LLL, giving Chef two t-shirts for LLL customers. The SSS t-shirt cannot be converted upward, so it remains unused.
